title: Obione pamirica
taxonomy: Obione pamirica is classified as a member of the family Amaranthaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the amaranth or pigweed family. It belongs to the genus Obione, which is a small genus of amaranth plants native to Central Asia. The family Amaranthaceae is further classified under the order Caryophyllales, which includes other plant families such as the carnation and the cactus. Within the genus Obione, Obione pamirica is one of several species found in the region. The classification of Obione pamirica reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Amaranthaceae family.

intro: Obione pamirica, also known as the Pamir plantain, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Amaranthaceae, native to Central Asia. The health benefits of Obione pamirica include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Obione pamirica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Obione pamirica is often used to make herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
